reply_text:
​@joaquimbarbosa896I wrote you a super long reply and received an error that it couldn't post.
First of all, why would a bot even hype Leo when we all know the bots would be hyping Qin? I don't use AI, especially not for a YT comment, lol. 

Ugh, let me see how i can summarize it without writing g that long comment again. If somehow the first one comes through sorry for the double reply.

My first read of r9 al.ost made me rage quit the manga.
For starters, I wasn't that big on either fighter. I am not a fan of characters who lean so heavy on profanity.
That and it felt like it was missing a couple of chapters.
When the ending happened, and I was getting into both fighters,  I felt cheated and had decided to drop it.

I decided to go back and reread it and it all clicked for me.
Especially the ending.
When I realized that in spite of his arrogance and proclaiming Leo would kneel, Apollo kneeled so he could stop Leonidas' knees from touching the ground and acknowledged his Valkyrie, Geirolul.  I finally got it. It also helped me understand Leo and all three became my favorites.

As for Leo, his reactions, personality and expressions really hit home this time. I've since read it a few more times and find all kinds of things to like about him. The arrow scene, the dismissive way he talked to Brunhilda and Goll...he is the opposite of someone like me, but I felt like Hagis, admiring him and his style.

video_title: Record of Ragnarok III | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text:
​@joaquimbarbosa896  
I also disliked r9 the first time I read it.
I almost rage quit the manga, in fact.

At first I didnt like Leo or Apollo because Leo seemed to just be angry man who cusses and Apollo was the pretty boy arrogant trope.

Then when I became invested in both, the match ended too abruptly. 
I felt cheated and debated dropping the manga and  just waiting for the anime.

I had already been put off by r7 getting g so much backstory for Qin and I liked r8 well enough, but had moved on.

video_title: Record of Ragnarok III | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text:
​@joaquimbarbosa896

Then I decided to reread r9 and see if I had missed something after seeing others talk about what they liked.

So I did. And I fell in love with r9.
The ending didnt make me rage this time, it made me cry.
I realized how Apollo who had said he would make Leo kneel, dropped to HIS knees so Leo's knees never touched the ground. I saw how he held Geirolul by the hand and acknowledged her.

I realized they were the perfect opponents for one another.

video_title: Record of Ragnarok III | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text:
​@joaquimbarbosa896as for his personality,  it grew on me. I felt like I was understanding him along gwith Apollo in the story and seeing beyond the superficiality of his rage.
The profanity and smoking were huge turnoff at first, but I grew to accept those as part of his personality. 

Calling him one note could easily apply to all the characters except for maybe Brunhilde, Apollo and Beel.
Most characters rely on either a character quirk or a single trait in this series. That doesnt make them badly written, and I would also say they all have several layers you have to uncover through a reread paying attention to the text and expressions. Even Lu Bu and Thor have more to them than just big brats who love to fight.


In the end, Apollo and Geirolul all be ame my favorites, probably top 3.
